_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning that different people can have different views about the same event or story from the past.

_source.w: We are learning that different people can have different views about the same event or story from the past.
y: Hearing different perspectives helps us understand that history has many sides.
Respecting other viewpoints teaches us to listen and think before we decide what we believe.
Understanding perspectives helps us realise that our own story is not the only one.
eg: Listening to two people tell the story of the same family event and noticing the differences.
Reading two different books about the same topic and comparing what they say.
Talking about why a grandparent might remember something differently from a parent.
Hearing a story about a local place from an Aboriginal Elder and from a non-Indigenous community member.
Discussing why different people might feel differently about a historical event.
Acting out a scene and retelling it from two different characters' points of view.
